per_trial.rationale: The plan excels across all dimensions. Hypothesis_statement provides three quantitative, falsifiable forms (point, slope, residual) with an error bound |Δq − ω·T| ≤ |ω|·Δt grounded in the actual 10 Hz controller_manager update_rate from diffbot_controllers.yaml. Controlled_variables specify concrete levels with a thorough confounds table including the velocity ramp, cmd_vel_timeout, and open_loop odom path. Recorded_signals justify each topic with rate and purpose, and success_thresholds derive numeric bounds (with units) directly from the hypothesis. The visualization_plan enumerates five distinct figures with specific signals and inspection criteria, and failure_modes lists six concrete pitfalls (ramp masking, wrong hardware plugin, TwistStamped mismatch, NaN init, etc.) each paired with a specific mitigation. Repo_grounding is exemplary, citing the exact Euler line in diffbot_system.cpp:192, the on_activate NaN init, throttled log lines, yaml line numbers, and the xacro mock-vs-real plugin distinction.

The hypothesis_statement is fully quantitative with an explicit error bound tied to the controller's 10 Hz update period (Δt = 0.1 s), and is broken into point/slope/residual forms with explicit refutation criteria. controlled_variables enumerates concrete IV levels (v, T, direction) plus a thorough confounds table covering the velocity ramp, update rate, cmd_vel_timeout, CPU load, and floating-point precision with hold-constant strategies. recorded_signals lists each topic with type, source, rate, and per-topic rationale, and explains why 10 Hz on /joint_states is the correct ceiling. success_thresholds are numeric with units and derived directly from the hypothesis (slope 1.000 ± 0.02, R² ≥ 0.999, residual bands of Δt·|ω|), the visualization_plan specifies five distinct plots with signals and inspection criteria, and failure_modes enumerates six concrete pitfalls (ramp, broadcaster rate, timeout, mock-hardware confusion, TwistStamped mismatch, NaN init) each with specific mitigations. repo_grounding cites exact file paths, line numbers, YAML keys, the read()/write() Euler step, hw_positions_/hw_velocities_, and open_loop:true semantics, demonstrating the plan is built on this specific runtime.

The plan provides three quantitative, falsifiable hypotheses (point/slope/residual forms) explicitly grounded in the 10 Hz update rate from diffbot_controllers.yaml, satisfying hypothesis_statement and success_thresholds with units derived from Δt·|ω|. Controlled_variables enumerate concrete IV levels plus a thorough confounds table (ramp, update_rate, cmd_vel_timeout, open_loop telemetry, FP precision). Recorded_signals justify each topic with rates and rationale tied to the integrator, and the visualization_plan specifies five distinct plots with signal-by-signal inspection criteria. Failure_modes lists six concrete risks with specific mitigations, and repo_grounding cites the exact Euler line in diffbot_system.cpp:192, hw_positions_/hw_velocities_, on_activate NaN handling, the xacro mock-hardware switch, and yaml line numbers — clearly tailored to example_2's actual runtime.
